body.title: First message of new day prompts to close previous day's slate
body.result: stale_slate_check.py + 11 unit tests + /context-load Step 0.25 wiring. Soft-prompt when prior-day slate has open items without ✅ CLOSED marker. Commit 17d7d0ac.
body.description:
When CC receives the first user message on a new calendar day and yesterday's (or older) slate has open items, soft-prompt: 'Last slate was YYYY-MM-DD — close it first? (y/defer/skip)'. If yesterday's slate is already fully closed (done/closed marker present), skip silently.

Affected files:
- New: detection helper in lab/claudecode/ (stale-slate check + open-items grep)
- ~/.claude/skills/context-load/SKILL.md or a SessionStart hook — wire the prompt

Design rules:
- This is a soft prompt, not a hard gate — 'defer'/'skip' both continue without running /day-close
- Skip silently when yesterday's slate has no open items (happy path is invisible)
- One-shot per new day — never fires twice in the same day

Scope:
- Detection: newest ~/.TheIgors/claudecode/*.slate.txt date vs today
- Open-items check: presence of '## Next up' or '## Blocked' items not marked ✅/closed
- Fires on first user message of new day (CC-self-imposed at session-start time)

Out of scope (separate tickets):
- Hard block (design: this is a prompt, not a gate)
- Mid-session midnight crossing (handle if/when it becomes a real problem)
- export_chat date-routing (T-export-chat-per-message-date-routing)

Test plan:
- Unit: date-detection helper + open-items detection (stale slate with items, stale slate fully closed)
- Manual: create stale slate with open items, start new session, verify prompt fires
- Manual: fully-closed slate, start new session, verify silent skip
- Manual: same day second message — verify does NOT re-prompt
